issuesFound.title: Table header cell missing scope attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The "Feature" table header cell lacks a scope attribute. Since the table contains multiple header cells in the header row, screen readers cannot definitively determine that this is a column header without the scope="col" attribute.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The email input field in the newsletter form has no associated <label> element, aria-label, or aria-labelledby attribute. Screen readers cannot identify the purpose of this field, failing WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships) and 3.3.2 (Labels or Instructions).
issuesFound.fix: Line 472: <input type="email" id="newsletter-email" name="email" autocomplete="email" required aria-required="true" placeholder="you@example.com" aria-label="Email address">

issuesFound.title: Logo bar section missing distinguishing label

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on secondary text
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p selector uses color #aaaaaa (var(--colour-text-secondary)) against a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text, making content difficult to read for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none and only adjusts outline-offset, which does not create a visible focus indicator. Keyboard users navigating the page will not see which element has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance.

issuesFound.title: Touch target at minimum size without compensating padding
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links have a min-height of 24px, which meets the minimum but falls below the 44px recommended size. No compensating padding of at least 8px is provided in the same rule, potentially making these links difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the default outline with outline: none and provides only an outline-offset, which renders no visible focus ring. This prevents keyboard users from seeing when buttons receive focus.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size with no padding
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit dimensions of 28px × 28px, which is below the 44px recommended size and has zero padding. This creates a touch target that may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate accurately.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size (24px)
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has explicit dimensions of 20px × 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum target size of 24×24 pixels. This makes it difficult for users with motor impairments or tremors to select the control.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size with insufficient padding
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets a min-height of 36px (within the 24–43px range) but only provides 4.5px of vertical padding (space-1-5), which is less than the 8px required to meet the 44px recommended touch target size. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating these small buttons.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property, creating spatial movement animations. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this transform transition for users who have requested reduced motion, potentially causing vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip-link class animates transform for its slide-in effect without a prefers-reduced-motion media query override. Users who have requested reduced motion may still see the sliding animation, which can cause vestibular discomfort.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The hero CTA button redeclares a transition including transform, creating spatial movement on hover/focus. No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule exists to disable this animation for users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This modifier class only changes the outline-offset but does not restore the outline removed by the base :focus-visible rule. Since the base rule sets outline: none, this results in no visible focus indicator when the inset variant is used.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which makes the content visible in the layout instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to assistive technology. issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on blockquote
issuesFound.explanation: The blockquote element uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) which resolves to #aaaaaa against the default background of #ffffff. This results in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.7:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px. Outline offset alone does not create a visible focus ring; with outline set to none, the offset applies to a zero-width outline and renders nothing. Keyboard users will have no visible indication of focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 293: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The universal :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset, removing the visible focus ring for all keyboard-focusable elements. Without a replacement such as box-shadow or border, keyboard users cannot see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast in blockquote text
issuesFound.explanation: The blockquote selector uses var(--colour-text-secondary) which is #aaaaaa against a white background (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 229: `color: var(--colour-neutral-600);` (or any colour that provides at least 4.5:1 contrast against white)

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast in filter label
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-label selector uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on a white background, providing only 2.3:1 contrast ratio, failing WCAG AA requirements for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 938: `color: var(--colour-neutral-600);`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast in section header paragraph
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p selector uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white background, resulting in 2.3:1 contrast which is below the required 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1043: `color: var(--colour-neutral-600);`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast in plan tagline
issuesFound.explanation: The .plan-tagline selector uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white background, providing insufficient contrast at 2.3:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1209: `color: var(--colour-neutral-600);`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast in price period text
issuesFound.explanation: The .price-period selector uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white background, failing WCAG AA contrast requirements with a ratio of approximately 2.3:1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1213: `color: var(--colour-neutral-600);`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast in input hint text
issuesFound.explanation: The .input-hint selector uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white background, resulting in 2.3:1 contrast which is below the 4.5:1 threshold required for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1232: `color: var(--colour-neutral-600);`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast in step content paragraph
issuesFound.explanation: The .step-content p selector uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white background, providing only 2.3:1 contrast ratio and failing WCAG AA.
issuesFound.fix: Line 925: `color: var(--colour-neutral-600);`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast in product body text
issuesFound.explanation: The .product-body > p:not(.product-tagline) selector uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white background, resulting in insufficient contrast of 2.3:1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1199: `color: var(--colour-neutral-600);`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast in footer brand paragraph
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-brand > p selector uses var(--colour-neutral-400) (#64748b) against var(--colour-neutral-900) (#0f172a) background, resulting in approximately 3.6:1 contrast which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1297: `color: var(--colour-neutral-300);` (or lighter)

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast in footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a selector uses var(--colour-neutral-400) (#64748b) on var(--colour-neutral-900) (#0f172a) background, providing only 3.6:1 contrast and failing WCAG AA for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1311: `color: var(--colour-neutral-300);`

issuesFound.title: Account navigation links rely on colour alone
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a selector inherits text-decoration: none and relies solely on colour change for hover state, failing to provide a non-colour visual differentiator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 694-696: Add `text-decoration: underline;` on hover or `border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor;`

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation links rely on colour alone
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a selector inherits text-decoration: none and uses only colour to distinguish links from surrounding text, violating WCAG 1.4.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1303-1305: Add `text-decoration: underline;` to the hover state or `border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor;`

issuesFound.title: Social links rely on colour alone
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a selector inherits text-decoration: none and relies solely on colour change to indicate interactivity, providing no non-colour visual indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1317-1319: Add `text-decoration: underline;` on hover or `border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor;`

issuesFound.title: Step content links rely on colour alone at rest
issuesFound.explanation: The .step-content p a selector sets colour to var(--colour-brand-primary) but inherits text-decoration: none, relying only on colour to distinguish links from surrounding text until hover.
issuesFound.fix: Line 932: Add `text-decoration: underline;` or `border-bottom: 1px solid var(--colour-brand-primary);`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector removes the default outline but does not provide a visible alternative like box-shadow or border in the same rule-block. Keyboard users will not see where focus is located.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on @media (forced-colors: active) :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, :focus-visible has outline removed without a visible replacement. High-contrast mode users will lose focus indication.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the outline but relies only on outline-offset, which is invisible without a non-zero outline. No box-shadow or border is provided.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a box-shadow or border. Keyboard users cannot identify focus on the navigation toggle.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links remove the outline on focus-visible without a visible alternative. Users navigating via keyboard cannot see which submenu item is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes the outline on focus-visible without a box-shadow or border. The focus state is invisible to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the outline on focus-visible without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot track focus in the account menu.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets a min-height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required for touch targets. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate small buttons.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width and min-height of 20px, below the 24px minimum. Icon buttons are difficult to tap accurately on touch screens.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #site-search button[type="submit"] 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is explicitly sized to 28px by 28px. While above the 24px minimum, it is below the recommended 44px size, potentially causing touch errors.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input is sized to 20px by 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Users may find it difficult to toggle the checkbox on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a 24px
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have a min-height of 24px. This meets the minimum but is below the recommended 44px size, increasing the risk of touch errors.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and clip: auto, which fails to hide content visually while keeping it available to screen readers. Content intended for AT users will be visible on screen.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses a transform transition for animation. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion) block is empty and does not disable this motion for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class uses a transform transition. The prefers-reduced-motion media query does not override this, causing unwanted motion for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses a 3D transform transition. The prefers-reduced-motion block at line 789 is empty and fails to disable this spatial animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .step
issuesFound.explanation: The .step element uses box-shadow and border-color transitions. The prefers-reduced-motion media query does not disable these transitions for this selector.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .testimonial-card
issuesFound.explanation: The .testimonial-card uses box-shadow and border-color transitions. No prefers-reduced-motion override exists for this selector, potentially causing discomfort.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .faq-item
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item uses border-color and box-shadow transitions. The prefers-reduced-motion override only targets the ::after pseudo-element, leaving the container motion active.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .form-group input
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs use border-color and box-shadow transitions. No prefers-reduced-motion override disables these transitions for input elements.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing to -0.2em. Negative word-spacing reduces the space between words, making text significantly harder to read for users with cognitive or visual disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .section-header p foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.2:1
issuesFound.explanation: The paragraph text uses #aaaaaa on a #ffffff background. The contrast ratio is approximately 2.2:1, which is well below the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .product-body > p foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.2:1
issuesFound.explanation: Product card body text uses #aaaaaa on #ffffff. The contrast ratio is too low for users with low vision to read comfortably.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .step-content p foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.2:1
issuesFound.explanation: Step content text uses #aaaaaa on #ffffff. This fails WCAG AA contrast requirements for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .plan-tagline foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.2:1
issuesFound.explanation: The plan tagline text uses #aaaaaa on #ffffff. The low contrast makes the text difficult to perceive.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .required-note foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.2:1
issuesFound.explanation: The required field note uses #aaaaaa on #ffffff. Important form instructions should have higher contrast to ensure they are read.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: blockquote foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.2:1
issuesFound.explanation: Blockquote text uses #aaaaaa on #ffffff. This fails the 4.5:1 contrast ratio requirement.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .logo-bar-label foreground #94a3b8 / background #f8fafc ratio 2.5:1
issuesFound.explanation: The logo bar label uses #94a3b8 on #f8fafc. The contrast ratio is approximately 2.5:1, which is below the 4.5:1 threshold.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .pricing-toggle > span foreground #aaaaaa / background #f8fafc ratio 2.2:1
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle label uses #aaaaaa on #f8fafc. This text is too light against the background for reliable readability.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .input-hint foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.2:1
issuesFound.explanation: Input hint text uses #aaaaaa on #ffffff. Helper text must meet contrast requirements to be accessible.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text: #nav-menu > li > a
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links have text-decoration: none and rely only on color changes on hover to indicate interactivity. Users with color vision deficiencies may not perceive them as links.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text: [id$="-submenu"] li a
issuesFound.explanation: Submenu links remove underlines and use only color/background changes on hover. This fails SC 1.4.1 Use of Color.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove underlines and rely on color alone to indicate interaction. This creates a barrier for users who cannot perceive color differences.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer links remove underlines and use only color changes on hover. Non-color indicators like underlines are required for accessibility.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text: .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links remove underlines and rely on color changes. Users with color blindness may not identify these as interactive links.

issuesFound.fix: Line 442: .features-section .section-header p { font-size: var(--font-size-lg); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); margin: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on pricing plan taglines
issuesFound.explanation: The .plan-tagline selector uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white background inside pricing cards, failing the 4.5:1 contrast requirement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1100: .plan-tagline { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-semibold); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); margin: 0; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-wide); }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none without providing a non-colour visual differentiator (such as underline, border-bottom, or font-weight change) for links in body text, causing links to be distinguishable only by color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 138: a { text-decoration: underline; } or alternatively add border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, which is not visible. Keyboard users will not see a focus ring on any element that relies on this default rule.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside @media (forced-colors: active), :focus-visible sets outline: none without a visible replacement. High-contrast mode users will lose focus indication.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset, which is invisible without a non-zero outline width. Button focus is hidden for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset. The mobile navigation toggle button will not show a focus ring.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset. Dropdown menu links will not show focus indication.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset. The search button focus is invisible.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account nav links
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset. Account navigation links will not show focus rings.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .nav-toggle 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle button has min-height and min-width of 28px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8).

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #nav-menu > li > a 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links in #nav-menu have min-height of 28px, below the 44px recommended touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: submenu links 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links have min-height of 28px, below the 44px recommended touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #search-input 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search input has height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: search submit button 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has width and height of 28px, below the 44px recommended touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: checkbox 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has width and height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required size (WCAG 2.5.8).

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, making content fully visible instead of hidden. This breaks the utility's purpose of hiding content visually while keeping it for screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transition: transform without a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable the motion for users who prefer it.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img uses transition: transform with 3D rotation. The existing media query at Line 899 is empty and does not disable this motion.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words and can significantly impair readability for users with cognitive or visual disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .feature-card p
issuesFound.explanation: Text color #aaaaaa on background #ffffff has a contrast ratio of 2.85:1, which fails the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text (WCAG 1.4.3).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .step-content p
issuesFound.explanation: Text color #aaaaaa on background #ffffff has a contrast ratio of 2.85:1, failing the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .product-body > p
issuesFound.explanation: Text color #aaaaaa on background #ffffff has a contrast ratio of 2.85:1, failing the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .input-hint
issuesFound.explanation: Text color #aaaaaa on background #ffffff has a contrast ratio of 2.85:1, failing the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector removes text-decoration (underlines). Body text links (e.g., inside FAQ answers) rely on colour alone to be identified as links in their default state.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the :focus-visible selector removes the outline (outline: none) and provides only outline-offset. In high-contrast mode, this leaves users with no visible focus indicator, violating WCAG 2.4.11.
issuesFound.fix: Line 364-367: `@media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset, which renders no visible focus ring for keyboard users. This violates WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance (AA) as there is no alternative box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline to indicate focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 351: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (line 586), which causes spatial movement on state changes. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this transform transition for users who prefer reduced motion, violating WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 579-590: `.btn { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: var(--space-2); min-height: 32px; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-5); font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-semibold); line-height: var(--line-height-tight); text-align: center; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap; cursor: pointer; user-select: none; border: 2px solid transparent; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); vertical-align: middle; position: relative; isolation: isolate; }` and add `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the default outline and provides only outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for button elements when navigated via keyboard.
issuesFound.fix: Line 591-594: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with zero padding, resulting in a touch target of 28×28 px. This is below the 24×24 px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (AA) and fails the 44×44 px recommendation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 820-827: `#site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on icon buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, creating a 20×20 px touch target below the 24 px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8.
issuesFound.fix: Line 950-957: `.btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkboxes
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] has width: 20px and height: 20px, creating a touch target below the 24 px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1416-1421: `.form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on account navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a selector inherits text-decoration: none (line 176) and the hover state only changes color, not providing a non-colour visual differentiator such as underline, border-bottom, or font-weight change. This relies solely on colour to distinguish links from surrounding text, violating WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 857-859: `nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:hover { color: var(--colour-brand-primary); text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on navigation menu links and buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The selector #nav-menu > li > a:focus-visible and #nav-menu > li > button:focus-visible removes outline without providing a visible alternative, leaving interactive navigation elements without a focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 774-777: `#nav-menu > li > a:focus-visible, #nav-menu > li > button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has a transition on transform (line 1004) that creates spatial movement on hover. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 1016-1019 is empty and does not override this transition, violating WCAG 2.3.3.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body text
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words to a negative value. This impairs readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, violating WCAG 1.4.8 Visual Presentation (AAA) and creating a barrier for many users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 147: Remove the line `word-spacing: -0.2em;` or change to `word-spacing: normal;`

issuesFound.fix: Line 253: body { word-spacing: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .features-section .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: The text color #aaaaaa on background #ffffff has a contrast ratio of 2.33:1, which is below the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text. Users with low vision may not read this text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 720: .features-section .section-header p { color: #555555; }

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation marks a field as invalid, it updates aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true". Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2 and 3.3.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3952: Insert `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` as the first line inside the function, before `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not set to "true". Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4382: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Closing the mobile navigation drawer emits a custom event but does not announce the state change to screen readers. Users navigating with assistive technology are not informed that the menu has closed, unlike when it opens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4420: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` immediately before the line `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown panel does not update the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". The _closeInstance function sets it to "false", but the open counterpart is missing, leaving screen readers unaware of the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4552: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus does not trigger live region announcements. Screen reader users receive no feedback when dropdown panels expand or collapse.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4556 (in _openInstance): Insert `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown opened');` after `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', ...);` Line 4576 (in _closeInstance): Insert `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown closed');` after `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', ...);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding a FAQ accordion item does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets it to "false", but the open() function omits this state change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 7052: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing focus management
issuesFound.explanation: Opening the mobile navigation drawer does not move keyboard focus into the drawer or activate the focus trap. Keyboard users remain on the trigger button, and the focus trap (_trap) is never activated despite being declared.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The open function in the mobileNav module sets the drawer to visible but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the navigation menu is now expanded.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The close function in the mobileNav module hides the navigation but does not announce the state change to screen readers via a live region, unlike the open function which does. Users may not realize the menu has closed.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function in the dropdownMenu module reveals the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This breaks the disclosure pattern for screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function in the dropdownMenu module changes visible content but does not announce the expansion to screen readers via a live region.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function in the dropdownMenu module hides the panel but does not announce the collapse to screen readers via a live region.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid to true
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function adds an aria-describedby reference but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates function uses aria-selected instead of aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates function sets aria-selected on filter buttons. Since these are standalone toggle buttons and not tabs within a tablist, aria-pressed is the correct state attribute.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The open function in the faqAccordion module expands the answer panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistency.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._onAccept function does not announce consent decision
issuesFound.explanation: The _onAccept function processes cookie consent but does not announce the acceptance to screen readers via a live region. Users may not confirm their choice was registered.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._onDecline function does not announce consent decision
issuesFound.explanation: The _onDecline function processes cookie consent but does not announce the declination to screen readers via a live region. Users may not confirm their choice was registered.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open function reveals the mobile navigation drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users cannot perceive that the menu has expanded, violating the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Line (inside mobileNav.open, after `_open = true;`): `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, no announcement is made to screen readers. Users are not notified that the menu has closed and that focus may have shifted.
issuesFound.fix: Line (inside mobileNav.close, before `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });`): `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function displays a dropdown panel but does not update the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". Assistive technologies cannot convey the expanded state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line (inside dropdownMenu._openInstance, after `inst.open = true;`): `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown menu updates the visual state but does not announce the expansion to screen readers, leaving users unaware that new content is available.
issuesFound.fix: Line (inside dropdownMenu._openInstance, after opening the panel): `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons toggle active states visually but do not update the aria-pressed attribute to reflect the pressed state to screen readers, failing to indicate which filter is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line (inside _syncTabAriaStates forEach, after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`): `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown menu does not announce the collapsed state to screen readers, which may confuse users who rely on auditory feedback to confirm their action.
issuesFound.fix: Line (inside dropdownMenu._closeInstance, before `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });`): `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel.togglePlay missing state announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Toggling the carousel play/pause state changes the button's visual label but does not announce the new state (playing or paused) to screen readers via the live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line (inside testimonialsCarousel.togglePlay, after `if (_playBtn) { setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', String(_playing)); }`): `liveRegion.announce(_playing ? STRINGS.carouselPlaying || 'Playing' : STRINGS.carouselPaused || 'Paused');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The function associates an error message with a field via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot identify the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line (inside srUtils.markInvalid, after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`): `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._showBanner missing announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The cookie consent banner appears visually but is not announced to screen reader users, who may not know that a dialog requiring their attention has appeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line (inside cookieConsent._showBanner, after `_bannerEl.removeAttribute('hidden');` or similar show logic): `liveRegion.announceAssertive(STRINGS.cookieBannerShown || 'Cookie consent banner shown. Please review your preferences.');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing focus management
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, focus is not moved to the drawer or the first focusable element within it. Keyboard users remain on the trigger button and cannot access the menu content.
issuesFound.fix: Line (inside mobileNav.open, after `_lockScroll();`): `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close missing focus return
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button. Keyboard users lose their place in the page focus order.
issuesFound.fix: Line (inside mobileNav.close, at end of function): `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._onOutsideClick missing focus return
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown closes due to a click outside the navigation, focus is not returned to the trigger button, causing focus to be lost to the document body.
issuesFound.fix: Line (inside dropdownMenu._onOutsideClick, before `_closeAll();`): `const openInst = _instances.find(i => i.open);` Line (after `_closeAll();`): `if (openInst) openInst.trigger.focus();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds CSS classes to show the drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to "false", causing a state desync where screen readers believe the menu is closed even when it is visually open.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function removes the hidden attribute from the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The corresponding _closeInstance sets it to "false", leaving assistive technology unaware that the dropdown is now expanded.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function animates and reveals the answer panel but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger. The close function sets it to "false", resulting in incorrect state reporting to screen readers when an item is expanded.

issuesFound.title: pricing _syncToggleState uses invalid aria-checked on button element
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._syncToggleState function sets aria-checked on _toggleEl. However, pricing.init only assigns role="switch" if the element is NOT a button. If a <button> is used, it retains role="button", where aria-checked is invalid (aria-pressed should be used instead).

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu module opens and closes navigation panels dynamically without announcing the state change to a live region. Unlike mobileNav which announces "Navigation menu opened/closed", dropdown users are not informed when a submenu expands or collapses.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent actions not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The cookieConsent._onAccept and _onDecline functions hide the banner and update analytics but do not announce the consent decision to screen readers. Users are not confirmed that their preference (Accept/Decline) has been successfully recorded.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open fails to set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in the mobileNav module adds CSS classes to show the navigation drawer but does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "true". Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu has expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2408: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); immediately after setting _open = true;

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function updates ARIA attributes and CSS classes to hide the mobile navigation drawer, but unlike the open() function, it does not announce the state change via liveRegion.announce(). Screen reader users are not notified that the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2430: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); inside the close function before or after emit.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance fails to set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function reveals the dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute and adding CSS classes, but it never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (inst.trigger). This prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2558: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inside _openInstance after inst.open = true;

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu opens, the _openInstance function emits a custom event but does not write to an aria-live region to announce the change to screen reader users. This is particularly important when the dropdown content is updated dynamically.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2558: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Dropdown opened'); inside _openInstance after opening the panel.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open fails to set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When opening an FAQ item, the open() function animates the panel height and removes the hidden attribute, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the item.trigger element. This leaves assistive technologies unaware that the accordion is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4208: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inside the open function after item.open = true;

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-pressed or aria-selected state
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates function toggles CSS classes for active filter tabs but does not set aria-pressed (for toggle buttons) or aria-selected (for tabs) to indicate the active state to screen reader users. The buttons appear as plain buttons without state information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3408: Add setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive)); or setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive)); inside the forEach loop after toggleClass.

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput validation does not set aria-invalid on the input field
issuesFound.explanation: When the discount code validation fails, the _onApply function announces the error via a live region and updates a status element, but it never sets aria-invalid="true" on the _inputEl itself. When validation succeeds, it does not clear aria-invalid. Screen readers will not identify the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4830: Add srUtils.markInvalid(_inputEl, _statusEl); when result.valid is false, and srUtils.markValid(_inputEl); when result.valid is true (or use setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');).

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function closes the dropdown panel and updates aria-expanded, but it does not announce the closure via a live region. Users navigating with screen readers may not realize the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2578: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Dropdown closed'); inside _closeInstance.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function collapses the FAQ panel and updates classes, but unlike the open() function, it does not announce the collapsed state via liveRegion.announce(). Screen reader users are not informed that the content has been hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4228: Add liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqClosed || '{q} — collapsed').replace('{q}', truncate(item.question, 60))); inside the close function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable for focus trapping and deactivates it in the close() function, but the init() and open() functions never initialize _trap with createFocusTrap() or call _trap.activate(). Keyboard focus is not trapped within the mobile navigation drawer when it is open, allowing users to tab behind the modal overlay.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2380: Initialize _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: true }); inside init() after _drawer is found. Line 2408: Add _trap.activate(); inside open() after _lockScroll();

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds the open class to the drawer and announces the state change, but it fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The corresponding close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating a state mismatch where screen readers are not informed that the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function removes the hidden attribute and adds the open class to the panel, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The _closeInstance function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", leaving the trigger's expanded state incorrect when the dropdown opens.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function animates the panel and announces the change, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger button. The close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", causing screen readers to announce the wrong state when a FAQ item is expanded.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance do not announce state changes
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance functions change the visibility of the dropdown panel but do not announce the state change to screen readers via the liveRegion module. Users relying on assistive technology are not informed when a menu opens or closes.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open and close do not manage aria-hidden on drawer element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and close functions toggle the visibility of the drawer using CSS classes but do not toggle aria-hidden on the _drawer element itself. If the drawer is hidden via transform or similar CSS without display:none, it remains in the accessibility tree when closed.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and mobileNav.close() functions add and remove CSS classes to show and hide the navigation drawer, but they do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the open/closed state. Screen reader users will not be informed whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3250-3265: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;` in the open function. Line 3290-3305: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `_open = false;` in the close function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter dynamic content update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters function updates the visible product cards, count badges, and empty state messaging when filters are applied, but it does not announce these changes to screen readers via a live region. Users who cannot see the screen will not be informed that the product list has been updated or how many results are now showing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4600-4650: After the filter animation completes (inside the filterAnimation.run callback), add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);` or similar appropriate message.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions in the dropdownMenu module show and hide the dropdown panels by manipulating the hidden attribute and CSS classes, but they fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to indicate the state change to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3400-3410: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` in _openInstance after `inst.open = true;`. Line 3420-3430: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` in _closeInstance after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: pricing dynamic content update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function updates price displays and billing period labels when users toggle between monthly and annual billing, but it does not announce these price changes to screen readers. Users will not be informed that the prices have updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4950-4980: After updating the plan cards, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing');` (or monthly equivalent based on the period).

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() and close() functions animate the panel height and manage the hidden attribute, but they do not synchronize the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect whether the accordion panel is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5300-5320: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` in the open function after `item.open = true;`. Line 5340-5360: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` in the close function after `item.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion dynamic content update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are expanded or collapsed, the open and close functions emit custom events but do not announce the state change to screen readers via the liveRegion utility. Users will not be informed that content has been revealed or hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5300-5320: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened ? STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question) : 'Answer expanded');` in the open function. Line 5340-5360: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed ? STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question) : 'Answer collapsed');` in the close function.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestionListRenderer.mount() function configures the search input as a combobox and wires aria-controls, but it fails to set the initial aria-expanded="false" state. The attribute is only updated when the suggestion list is shown or hidden, leaving it undefined initially.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4150-4180: After `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`, add `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not establish an aria-controls relationship between the trigger button and the navigation drawer panel. Screen reader users cannot programmatically determine which element is controlled by the toggle button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3200-3240: After ensuring the drawer has an ID (using `srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav')`), add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id);` to link the trigger to the drawer.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close() function removes the open CSS class from the drawer element, but it does not set the hidden attribute or aria-hidden="true" to ensure the drawer is removed from the accessibility tree while it is visually hidden. Screen reader users may still be able to navigate to off-screen drawer content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3290-3310: Add `_drawer.setAttribute('hidden', '');` or `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` after removing the open class, and remove these attributes in the open function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function queries the trigger button and attaches event listeners, but it does not initialize the aria-expanded attribute to "false" when the menu is closed on page load. Screen readers will not know the initial state of the toggle.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3200-3240: After `_initialised = true;`, add `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu dynamic content update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels without announcing these visibility changes to screen readers. Users navigating with assistive technologies will not be informed when a submenu appears or disappears.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3400-3410: Add `liveRegion.announce('Submenu opened');` in _openInstance. Line 3420-3430: Add `liveRegion.announce('Submenu closed');` in _closeInstance (or use the STRINGS object for localization).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger does not update aria-expanded on toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module's open() and close() functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but fail to update the aria-expanded state on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen reader users will not be informed whether the menu is currently open or closed when activating the toggle.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1385: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1405: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function initializes the trigger element (_trigger) but does not set an initial aria-expanded="false" attribute. Assistive technologies may not correctly announce the initial closed state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1445: if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls reference
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not associate the trigger button (_trigger) with the controlled navigation drawer (_drawer) using aria-controls. Screen reader users cannot easily navigate from the trigger to the menu content.

issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle buttons are processed to set aria-pressed based on current preference, but if the buttons are created dynamically after init, they may not have the attribute set.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4745: Ensure setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(btnTheme === pref)); is called for all buttons including dynamically added ones (add MutationObserver or delegate).

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button missing aria-label initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button is selected from the DOM but the code does not ensure it has an aria-label if the HTML lacks one.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2528: Add if(_btn && !_btn.getAttribute('aria-label')) setAttr(_btn, ARIA.LABEL, STRINGS.scrollToTop || 'Scroll to top');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion category headers missing aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: mediaPlayerController missing aria-live for status
issuesFound.explanation: Status announcements (Playing, Paused, Muted) are sent to liveRegion.announce, but the media element itself lacks a dedicated aria-live region for its status, relying on the global region.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure liveRegion.announce is sufficient (it is), but verify the liveRegion is initialized (it is in Phase 1).

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator error message missing aria-live
issuesFound.explanation: The error message element is created with role="alert" and aria-live="polite", but some screen readers may not announce the error if the element is hidden initially and shown later without content change. The content is set before removing hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4175: Ensure errorEl.removeAttribute('hidden') is called after errorEl.textContent is set (it is), and consider using aria-live="assertive" for errors.

issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function adds an aria-describedby reference but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid, only that it has a description. This affects all forms using inlineValidator (newsletter, contact, wizard).

issuesFound.title: productFilter changes not announced to screen readers

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The open() and close() functions animate the FAQ answer panels but do not programmatically set aria-expanded="true" or aria-expanded="false" on the trigger buttons (item.trigger). This leaves screen reader users unaware of the current expanded or collapsed state of accordion items.

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced to screen readers

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons lack aria-pressed or aria-selected
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates() function toggles visual active states using CSS classes but does not set the aria-pressed attribute (for toggle buttons) or aria-selected attribute (for tabs) on the filter tab buttons. Assistive technologies cannot convey which filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3380: Inside the forEach loop, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` or `setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));` to communicate the active state.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() function removes error message associations but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute or set it to "false", leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1960: Insert `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` inside the markValid function after clearing aria-describedby.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not initialize the trigger button with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads. The initial collapsed state of the navigation drawer is not programmatically determinable by assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2410: After `_initialised = true;` and event listener attachments, insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not set an aria-controls attribute on the trigger button to reference the navigation drawer element (_drawer), preventing screen readers from understanding the relationship between the trigger and the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2410: After obtaining _drawer, insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id || srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not return focus to trigger on close
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed (via the close() function), focus is not programmatically returned to the trigger button that originally opened it. Keyboard users are left at the bottom of the DOM or in an unknown focus location.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2470: Insert `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();` inside the close() function before or after `_unlockScroll();`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs lack keyboard activation
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter module binds click events to filter tabs but provides no keyboard handler for Enter or Space activation. Combined with the use of roving tabindex, keyboard users can navigate to tabs but cannot activate them if they are not native <button> elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3350: Add `_tabsEl.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { const btn = e.target.closest('[data-filter-value]'); if (btn) { e.preventDefault(); _onTabClick({target: btn}); } } });`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded or aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer and overlay but fail to update the aria-expanded state on the trigger button or the aria-hidden state on the drawer. This prevents screen reader users from knowing the menu's open/closed state or accessing the drawer when visually hidden.

Line 1693: function open(announceToSR) { ... setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'false'); ... }
  Line 1713: function close(announceToSR) { ... setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true'); ... }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function sets up the navigation trigger but does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute on the _trigger element. Screen readers will not announce the initial closed state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1763: function init() { ... if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); ... }

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded or aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance functions manage the visibility of dropdown panels but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger or aria-hidden on the panel. This breaks the disclosure pattern for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:

This affects contactForm, newsletterForm, and multiStepWizard.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function links an error message to a field via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen readers and assistive technologies cannot identify that the field contains an invalid value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1495: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` inside the markInvalid function before or after addDescribedBy.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The open function for FAQ items animates the panel open and removes the hidden attribute, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger. Only the close function sets it to false, leaving the state out of sync when opening.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3820: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` in the open function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer open and close functions add and remove CSS classes to show/hide the drawer, but do not toggle aria-expanded on the hamburger trigger button. Assistive technologies cannot announce the state of the navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2220: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` inside the open function. Line 2240: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` inside the close function.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch toggle functions missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The site search combobox _open and _close functions manage the visibility of the suggestion listbox but do not update aria-expanded on the search input element. This prevents screen readers from announcing when suggestions are available.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2920: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` inside _open. Line 2940: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` inside _close.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters function dynamically updates the product grid, count badges, and empty states when filters change, but does not announce these changes via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed that the content has updated or how many results are now visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3180: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : \`Showing \${s.activeCategory} products\`);` after the filter animation completes.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The setPeriod function dynamically updates price values across all plan cards when the billing cycle changes, but provides no auditory feedback via a live region. Users relying on screen readers are unaware that prices have changed or which billing period is now active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3370: Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'month' ? STRINGS.pricingMonthly : STRINGS.pricingAnnual);` after updating the plan cards.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu init missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The register function in dropdownMenu initializes new dropdown instances by setting aria-haspopup and aria-controls, but fails to set the initial aria-expanded="false" state on triggers. Screen readers assume an undefined expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2500: Add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` inside the register function after setting aria-controls.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The open and close functions for FAQ items toggle the visibility of answers but do not announce the state change to screen readers, despite having announcement strings defined in STRINGS. Users are not informed when an accordion item expands or collapses.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3825: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question));` in open. Line 3875: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.question));` in close.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid removal
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid utility function removes the error description from aria-describedby but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute. Fields remain marked as invalid after being corrected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1515: Add `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` or `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` inside the markValid function.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch init missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch init function configures the search input as a combobox with aria-controls and aria-haspopup, but omits setting the initial aria-expanded="false" state. The input incorrectly appears to have an open suggestion list by default.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3060: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` inside the init function after setting other ARIA attributes.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav init function queries the navigation toggle button but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on page load. The button lacks the proper initial state before any interaction occurs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2290: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` inside the init function after _trigger is assigned.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open and close functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not announce these state changes via the live region, despite having STRINGS.announce.navOpen and navClosed available. Screen reader users are not notified when the menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2225: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);` in open. Line 2245: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);` in close.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap not instantiated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable and references it in open/close functions, but never creates a focus trap instance using createFocusTrap. Keyboard users can tab out of the open navigation drawer into the background content, violating WCAG focus containment requirements for modal dialogs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2295: Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });` inside init after _drawer is assigned. Ensure _trap.activate() is called in open and _trap.deactivate() in close.

issuesFound.title: Contact form message textarea broken aria-describedby reference
